user_message: Karen Patel at VanArsdel Ltd just sent this. Help me build the narrative.

Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-08 16:30 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: VanArsdel Ltd escalation triage

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for VanArsdel Ltd: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

handoffs.condition: contact and opportunity records need updating

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF38-B trap (Invents replacement-candidate); produces complete Stakeholder map delta, opportunity-at-risk assessment, re-engagement plan, contact update proposals,
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF38-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF38-B failure mode (Invents replacement-candidate)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
